title: Walkthrough: Settings — every key, a proper editor

beats.caption: Every settings.json key now has a typed editor — 82 keys across 15 groups
beats.expectedScreen: Settings page: left nav with a search box, '82 settings' count and group list; Model & reasoning rows with typed controls

beats.caption: Live search filters the whole catalog — one match: the Sandbox group
beats.expectedScreen: the nav showing '1 match' and only the Sandbox group section remaining in the content column

beats.caption: Permission rules are structured rows — tool + specifier, not a raw JSON blob
beats.expectedScreen: the Permissions section with Allow rules rendered as select+input rows (Bash / git add:*) and an Add rule control

beats.caption: Add an allow rule — validated against the official pattern, autosaved instantly
beats.expectedScreen: the Allow rules list with a NEW second Bash row (the added rule) highlighted under the seeded git add:* row

beats.caption: Object-forms edit nested keys — sandbox.enabled saves the whole object, unknown subkeys preserved
beats.expectedScreen: the Sandbox group object-form with its Enabled checkbox highlighted at the top of the form

beats.caption: Managed-only keys are honest — they only bind in IT's managed-settings.json
beats.expectedScreen: the expanded Enterprise / managed-only group with its warning banner and managed-only pills on rows

beats.caption: Bespoke keys Garrison does not know still round-trip untouched
beats.expectedScreen: the Advanced — unmanaged keys section with advisorModel and autoDreamEnabled shown as raw JSON

segments.subtitle: merge-managed · patch only what you touch · never clobber
